title: On the Ground in Beirut
summary: Our Beirut bureau chief, Christina Goldbaum, reports on the escalating conflict between Israel and the Iran-backed armed group Hezbollah, as Israel’s military seizes areas of southern Lebanon and carries out bombings.

title: Trump Wants a Quick Victory in Iran. But the War May Be Costly.
summary: The opening days of the conflict are challenging the idea that President Trump can project force abroad while safeguarding American lives and the economy.

title: Iran’s Friends Include China and Russia. But Where are They Now?
summary: Iran maintains ties with a range of countries, including Turkey, India, Russia and China. Yet in this war, their support is mostly rhetoric.

title: Sri Lanka Is Caught in the Middle as Second Iranian Vessel Seeks Safe Haven
summary: A day after rescuing Iranian sailors from the wreckage of a warship sunk by a U.S. submarine, Sri Lanka’s neutrality is being tested.

title: States Sue to Stop Trump From Reviving Steep Tariffs
summary: The lawsuit filed by two dozen states seeks to invalidate the president’s new, 10 percent global tax on imports.

title: Senator Tim Sheehy Helps Officers Forcibly Remove Protesting Veteran From Hearing
summary: The physical struggle, involving Senator Tim Sheehy of Montana, was captured on video. The protester, Brian McGinnis, said on social media that his arm was broken.

title: What to Know About the Texas Runoffs in May: Key Races and Candidates
summary: Texas voters will revisit the Republican Senate primary — and some House races where no candidate captured more than 50 percent of the vote — in runoffs on May 26.

title: Primaries Offer Warning Signs to Incumbents in Both Parties
summary: Even as some top targets held on, lawmakers in both parties were pushed into runoffs by challengers in Texas, while some in the North Carolina state legislature lost.

title: An Assertive Supreme Court Turns to Curbing State Courts
summary: Liberal justices accused their colleagues of expanding use of the emergency docket again in two orders issued this week.

title: A Judge’s ‘Battle Royale’ With Trump and the Supreme Court
summary: After repeated but cryptic rebukes from the justices, Judge Brian Murphy last week again ruled against one of the administration’s signature immigration programs.

title: How Jonathan Groff of ‘Just in Time’ Became Broadway’s Leading Man
summary: By transmitting his love of live performance, the “Just in Time” actor has completed his ascendance to full musical stardom.

title: F.D.A. Faces Upset Over Denials of New Drugs
summary: Agency officials promise fast reviews of new treatments while vowing they will not be a “rubber stamp” for the industry. But patients with rare diseases view recent decisions as signs that the doors are closing on their options.

title: 50 Medical Schools Back Kennedy Plan on Nutrition After Pressure
summary: Robert F. Kennedy Jr. announced the commitments in Washington, even as some in the medical community questioned whether the government should try to influence curriculums.

title: South African President on Trump’s Oval Office ‘Ambush’ and ‘Racist’ Policies
summary: In an interview with The New York Times, President Cyril Ramaphosa opened up about the role of middle powers, relations with Washington and apartheid.

title: Demand for AI Data Centers Sends Prospectors Hunting for Land and Power
summary: Brian Janous, a former Microsoft executive, and his firm Cloverleaf have become modern-day land men, packaging electricity and land for data centers.

title: A Word to the Wise: Don’t Trust A.I. to File Your Taxes
summary: The world’s smartest technology is no match for the U.S. tax code.

title: The Smart Home Never Quite Worked. Now It’s Getting an A.I. Reboot.
summary: Amazon and Google think that artificially intelligent assistants like Alexa+ and Gemini will speed up the process of setting up a smart home, but many problems remain unsolved.

title: How $800 Monthly Car Payments Are Hurting Car Sales
summary: Cars have become so expensive that many Americans are putting off or not buying new cars, hurting the auto industry.

title: Should New York City Burn Its Parks? This Scientist Thinks So.
summary: An unusual outbreak of wildfires in city parks gave scientists a chance to study these rare events. Now they’re coming to different conclusions.

title: L.A. Broker and Reality TV Star Accuses Oren Alexander of Sexual Assault
summary: Tracy Tutor said Mr. Alexander, a former top real estate agent in New York, drugged and assaulted her in 2014. Mr. Alexander and his two brothers are on trial for sex trafficking.

title: Deliberations Start in Sex-Trafficking Trial of Alexander Brothers
summary: Prosecutors called more than 30 witnesses, including 11 women who said the three men had sexually abused them. The brothers, who have pleaded not guilty, face life in prison if convicted.

title: Suspect Is in Custody After 3 Women Are Found Dead in Utah
summary: A suspect was detained in Colorado a day after two women were found dead on a hiking trail, and a third at a residence in Wayne County, the authorities said.

title: At the Whitney, a Biennial Gets Personal
summary: For an unmoored time, 56 artists and teams present an inspired discourse shaped by crisis, craft and community. Look up, and listen.

title: Does the Iran War Put America First?
summary: How the antiwar right sees Trump’s foreign policy.

title: Mass Hysteria. Thousands of Jobs Lost. Just How Bad Is It Going to Get?
summary: Michael Steinberger on how A.I.’s impact on white-collar jobs may transform politics and society.

title: Britney Spears Is Arrested on Suspicion of D.U.I. in California
summary: “Hopefully this can be the first step in long overdue change that needs to occur in Britney’s life,” a representative for the pop star said after she was released from jail.

title: Lawsuit Accuses Amy Griffin, Best-Selling Author, of Invasion of Privacy
summary: A woman contends that Amy Griffin’s story of being sexually abused, as described in “The Tell,” was based on assaults that she herself suffered.

title: Savannah Guthrie Plans a Return to ‘Today’
summary: Ms. Guthrie visited 30 Rockefeller Plaza on Thursday for the first time since her mother, Nancy, went missing in early February.

title: McDonald’s C.E.O. Takes a Big Bite Out of a Burger. Maybe Scratch ‘Big.’
summary: Chris Kempczinski’s rather tentative chomp drew mirth online, even from some competitors.
